message.content: {"post_number":4,"summary":"I’m exploring whether non‑standard build targets should be standardized, noting that they’re mainly for distribution tools like shiv, not pip installs. I’m uncertain if developers will adopt them, and I’d love feedback from frontend teams on whether this effort is worthwhile.","conversational_intent":"proposing and seeking feedback","stance":"uncertain","technical_burden":"low","phrase_learning_potential":"high","important_uncertainty":"I can't definitively say this one way or the other","phrase_candidates":[{"text":"I can't definitively say this one way or the other","function":"expressing uncertainty about the direction"},{"text":"I would love to get some feedback","function":"seeking feedback from others"},{"text":"Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem","function":"expressing self-doubt about feasibility"}]}
message.thinking:
We need to produce JSON with fields. Summarize the post: 1-2 short sentences, <=55 words. Preserve stance, uncertainty, qualification, conversational intent. He notes users may stay with old build system. He lists examples of projects using setuptools.Command. He says frontends like uv, hatch, pdm may support. He wonders if too demanding, maybe operate on built wheels. He wants feedback from frontend devs.

We need to capture stance: uncertain, exploratory, seeking feedback. Conversational intent: proposing, seeking feedback, evaluating. Technical burden: low? He lists examples but not deep. Probably low. Phrase learning potential: high? He uses phrases like "I can't definitively say", "I feel as though", "I would love to get some feedback". So high.

Important uncertainty: maybe "I can't definitively say this one way or the other" or "I feel as though users who need to create custom targets have probably just stayed with their old build system." But we need a single string or null. Provide the most important uncertainty. Let's choose: "I can't definitively say this one way or the other" or "I feel as though users who need to create custom targets have probably just stayed with their old build system." Which is more important? The first expresses uncertainty about the direction. The second expresses uncertainty about user behavior. I'd pick the first.

Phrase candidates: up to 3. Must be copied exactly from authored_text. Must contain reusable conversational wording, not only technical terms. Examples: "I can't definitively say this one way or the other", "I would love to get some feedback", "Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem". These are good. "I would love to get some feedback" appears.

"Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem" appears.

So phrase: "I can't definitively say this one way or the other" exactly.

"I would love to get some feedback from any of these frontend developers if this is something they can see being standardized." So phrase: "I would love to get some feedback" exactly.

"Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem" appears: "Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem and instead this sort of process should operate on already built `.whl`s like how [PyApp](https://github.com/ofek/pyapp) works". So phrase: "Maybe I'm demanding too much within the constraints of the build ecosystem" exactly.

Good.
